target.title: fix(device-pairing): guard role normalization against non-string entries
target.body:
## Summary

`normalizeRoleList` in `src/shared/device-pairing-access.ts` called `.trim()` on every `roles[]` entry and the singular `role` **without a `typeof === "string"` guard**. The `roles`/`role` fields are loaded from disk via a blind cast (`coercePairingStateRecord`, `src/infra/pairing-files.ts`), so a malformed/legacy pairing record with a non-string entry — e.g. `roles: [123]` or `role: 5` — threw `TypeError: role.trim is not a function`, crashing `resolvePendingDeviceApprovalState`. `openclaw devices list` calls that per pending request with no surrounding try/catch (`src/cli/devices-cli.runtime.ts`), so the whole table render blows up on one bad record.

The scopes half of the same summary (`normalizeDeviceAuthScopes`) is already guarded, and the merged #90654/#92178 work added the same guard to the sibling `mergeRoles`/`mergeScopes`/`formatAuditList` in `src/infra/device-pairing.ts` — but **missed this path**.

This routes each item through the existing shared non-string-safe normalizer `normalizeUniqueSingleOrTrimmedStringList` (the exact helper `mergeRoles` uses), so non-string entries are dropped instead of crashing while valid roles are still trimmed, deduped, and sorted. Net **−10 LOC**; all three `normalizeRoleList` callers (pending request, approved device, token roles) are covered by the single fix.

### Changes
- `src/shared/device-pairing-access.ts` — route `normalizeRoleList` through the guarded shared helper
- `src/shared/device-pairing-access.test.ts` — regression tests for non-string `roles` / `role` / `token.role`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `normalizeRoleList` crashed (`TypeError: role.trim is not a function`) on a non-string role from a malformed pairing record, taking down `resolvePendingDeviceApprovalState` / `openclaw devices list`; it now drops non-string entries and classifies access normally.

Real environment tested: a direct-call harness importing the real `src/shared/device-pairing-access.ts`, run with the repo's `tsx`, Node 22.22.1, no model / provider / channel credentials and no network (the function is pure). The "before" column reverts only that one source file to `origin/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# dp-proof.mts (~20 lines) imports resolvePendingDeviceApprovalState from the real source
# and calls it with malformed (non-string) roles/role on the pending and paired paths.
tsx dp-proof.mts                                                                      # AFTER (this branch)
git stash push -- src/shared/device-pairing-access.ts && tsx dp-proof.mts && git stash pop   # BEFORE (origin/main)
```

Evidence after fix:
```
########## AFTER FIX (this branch) ##########
OK     pending roles:[123]         -> {"kind":"new-pairing","requested":{"roles":[],"scopes":["operator.read"]},"approved":null}
OK     pending singular role:5     -> {"kind":"new-pairing","requested":{"roles":[],"scopes":["operator.read"]},"approved":null}
OK     paired (approved) roles:[7] -> {"kind":"role-upgrade","requested":{"roles":["operator"],"scopes":["operator.read"]},"approved":{"roles":[],"scopes":["operator.read"]}}
OK     valid+malformed mixed [123,'operator',null,'  admin  '] + role:5 -> {"kind":"new-pairing","requested":{"roles":["admin","operator"],"scopes":["operator.read"]},"approved":null}

########## BEFORE FIX (origin/main) ##########
THROW  pending roles:[123]         -> TypeError: role.trim is not a function
THROW  pending singular role:5     -> TypeError: item.trim is not a function
THROW  paired (approved) roles:[7] -> TypeError: role.trim is not a function
THROW  valid+malformed mixed ...   -> TypeError: role.trim is not a function
```

Observed result after fix: every non-string role entry that previously threw is now dropped, valid roles are trimmed/deduped/sorted (`["admin","operator"]`), and the approval classification returns normally — no crash on any of the three role paths.

What was not tested: end-to-end `openclaw devices list` with a hand-crafted on-disk pairing file holding a non-string role. The pure-function repro exercises the exact crash; reachability is confirmed by source (the `roles`/`role` fields reach `normalizeRoleList` unvalidated through `coercePairingStateRecord`'s blind cast, and the CLI's `parseDevicePairingList` only does `Array.isArray` checks, never element-type checks).

## Additional checks

Four cases in `device-pairing-access.test.ts` cover non-string `roles[]`, singular `role`, and `token.role` (exercising all three callers), alongside the pre-existing valid-role and revoked-token cases. Formatter, static analysis, and type checks all pass for the changed files. Mirrors the merged #90654/#92178 robustness fix; net −10 source LOC.

commands.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

target.title: feat(browser): My Browser node-anchored turn routing (stacks on #93411)
target.body:
> **Stacked on #93411 — that must merge first.** Builds on the run-scoped `gateway.tools.byNode` primitive from #93411; the diff currently overlaps #93411's commits and will be rebased to the extension-only delta once #93411 lands. Draft until then.

## What this is for

**The headline use case: fill the long, annoying forms & questionnaires for me.** You're logged into some portal — a multi-page application, an expense claim, a detailed survey — and you'd rather not hand-type 30 fields. My Browser keeps you in your *own* Chrome (your session, your cookies, your tabs) and lets you tell the side-panel agent, in plain English, what you want; it fills the form in your real browser, **self-checks its work**, and stops short of submitting so you review first. The same loop draws on natural language, the agent's OpenClaw **memory** (your saved details), and **web lookup** for fields it needs to recall or research.

*Proven end-to-end* — one instruction ("fill this pizza order: Cameron Beeley, +44 7700 900142, …, large, bacon + extra cheese, 19:00, comment 'ring the doorbell twice', don't submit") → the agent navigated, filled **every field type** (text, radio, checkboxes, time, textarea), **snapshotted to verify its own work** ("Let me take a screenshot to verify the text fields too"), and left it unsubmitted for review:

![Form-fill demo — from one natural-language instruction the agent fills a multi-field form (text, radio, checkboxes, time, textarea) in the user's real browser, self-verifies via snapshots, and leaves it unsubmitted](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-formfill.png)

Everything below is the plumbing + hardening that makes this safe to run in your real browser. See **User-story traceability** (after the findings) for how each fix protects this use case.

## Summary

Makes "My Browser" a usable side-panel copilot on top of node-anchored tool-scoping. Four blocks:

**1. Node-anchored turn routing (the original core).** Routes the side-panel turn **through the hosting node** as an authenticated node-originated `agent.request`, so the gateway confines that turn's tools via `gateway.tools.byNode` (#93411), and pins the browser proxy back to that same node. Falls back to a direct gateway turn when no node hosts the bridge.
- Node-host event emitter (`node-event-emitter.ts`, re-exported via `plugin-sdk/gateway-runtime`); extension bridge forwards the side-panel `agent.request` via an `onAgentRequest` callback threaded from `server-lifecycle`; browser tool pins to the turn's `hostingNodeId`.

**2. Browser-copilot persona as a system prompt.** `server-lifecycle` injects `BROWSER_COPILOT_SYSTEM_PROMPT` as `extraSystemPrompt` on every node-originated side-panel `agent.request` (server-side, not client-injectable). Without it the agent tends to refuse browser tasks / fetch over HTTP; with it, it acts through the browser tool, defers only CAPTCHA/2FA/payment, and won't reload a page it's already on.

**3. Side-panel UX (`sidepanel.js`/`background.js`).**
- **Per-tab agent sessions** — deterministic key per pinned tab (`…:thread:tab-<id>`), so each attached tab has its own resumable conversation; "+ New session" resets the thread.
- **Live tab-context injection** — each turn carries the pinned tab's current URL+title, so the agent knows where it is without a tool round-trip and won't re-navigate a page it's on.
- **Streaming render fixes** — render the gateway's authoritative full text (no duplicate/garbled deltas), render line breaks, and split inter-tool commentary into separate persisted bubbles with a `→ <tool>` step line per tool call (the panel opts into `tool-events`).

**4. Bridge reliability.**
- **Page-only target announce** — only real `page` targets are announced to Playwright; worker/`blob:`/service-worker/cross-origin-iframe child targets are dropped (they were announced as phantom pages, hanging the next snapshot ~47s on heavy pages like signup flows).
- **Per-turn CDP keepalive** — reuse the cached CDP connection within an idle TTL, force a fresh reconnect after a gap; skip the per-op throwaway health-probe when a live connection is cached.
- **Idempotent `attachTab` + `getTargetId`** — re-attaching an already-connected tab no longer crashes Playwright with a Duplicate-target error; the panel can resolve a tab's CDP targetId to pin turns to it.

Out of scope: the `gateway.tools.byNode` primitive itself (#93411). The node-bridge driver uses its own literal `extension-bridge`, distinct from the legacy `extension` → `existing-session` value (whose doctor migration is preserved), so there is no deprecation collision.

## Linked context

Depends on #93411 (gateway-core `byNode`). Related #93347 (closed — superseded by the authenticated-node approach).

## Review findings addressed (local ClawSweeper / Codex review, head 11b72146a8)

Local + public ClawSweeper / Codex review rounds flagged the items below; all are resolved or explained:

1. **[HIGH] Unauthenticated extension bridge** (`extension-bridge-server.ts`) — **fixed.** When `gateway.auth.token` is configured, the `/extension` relay handshake must present a matching HMAC on `?token=` (recomputed server-side via WebCrypto, mirroring the extension's `deriveRelayToken`) or the socket is rejected (close 1008); tokenless loopback stays trusted-local. Proven live on the deployed build — `no-token → 1008`, `wrong-token → 1008`, `correct-HMAC → accepted` — plus 4 unit tests (`extension-bridge-server.auth.test.ts`).
2. **[MED] Hard-coded `DEFAULT_TOKEN`** (`sidepanel.js`) — **fixed.** Removed `test-token-for-container`; tokenless loopback now omits auth instead of sending a placeholder credential.
3. **[high] node-event emission on the public SDK** (`plugin-sdk/gateway-runtime.ts`) — **fixed** (`04d6d08b3b`). `emitNodeGatewayEvent` (and the install primitives) are removed from the public plugin-SDK barrel and moved to a dedicated **private** entrypoint `node-gateway-events-internal` (mirroring `ssrf-runtime-internal`): built for the bundled first-party bridge but excluded from public package exports, so a third-party plugin cannot install an emitter or forge node-attributed events. The extension package-boundary checks (`no-plugin-sdk-internal`, `no-src-outside-plugin-sdk`, `subpaths-exported`) all pass. (The private-subpath form as first implemented broke **runtime** resolution — found via live deploy, fixed in `b3e840c785`; see #14.)
4. **[P1] macOS toggle writes invalid config** (`ConfigStore.swift`) — **fixed.** Added `browser.priorDefaultProfile` to the config schema so the My Browser toggle's state write validates against the strict `browser` object.
5. **[P1] `driver:"extension"` collided with the legacy migration** — **fixed** (`677c875a8e`). The branch had repurposed the legacy `driver:"extension"` literal (which the doctor migrates to `existing-session`, Chrome MCP attach) for the new node-bridge mode *and* deleted that migration, so the doctor would silently rewrite a new-mode profile and legacy upgrades broke. Fixed by giving the new mode its own literal **`extension-bridge`** (zod union + refine, public type, the input-side resolver, the macOS toggle) and **restoring** the deleted `extension → existing-session` profile migration. `doctor-legacy-config` migration test passes; build clean. (The rename initially left the relay gate on the dead `extension` literal — found via live deploy, fixed in `f4f56efa2e`; see #15.)
6. **[P1] Dependency-graph change** (`pnpm-lock.yaml`) — **stacked-base drift; rebase/approval-gated.** The bundled `chrome-extension/package.json` is `private` and adds **no runtime dependencies**; the only lockfile changes are upstream override bumps (`@anthropic-ai/sdk`, `hono`, `aws`, `acp`) carried by the #93411-stacked base, not this PR. They disappear on the rebase to the extension-only delta. Full resolution needs that rebase or maintainer dependency-guard approval — neither available pre-#93411.
7. **[P2] `config.example.json` relayPort** — **fixed** (`debaa45e3e`). Was `18790`; corrected to `18792` (`DEFAULT_EXTENSION_BRIDGE_PORT`) so the bundled example dials the right bridge port.
8. **[P2] relay URL throws under tokenless auth** (`background-utils.js`) — **fixed** (`e1d56a8aba`). `buildRelayWsUrl` threw on a blank gateway token, but `gateway.auth.mode:"none"` is valid; it now returns a tokenless `/extension` URL (the node bridge accepts it only when it also has no configured token, so a token-protected bridge still rejects it).
9. **[P1] Schema-invalid macOS profile** (`ConfigStore.swift:178`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). The My Browser toggle wrote `{driver:"extension-bridge"}` but the zod profile schema requires `color`; the toggle now writes a `color` so the saved config validates. (Distinct from #4, which added `priorDefaultProfile`.)
10. **[P1] Copilot prompt dropped** (`server-node-events.ts`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). `server-lifecycle` emits `extraSystemPrompt` on the node-originated `agent.request`, but the receiver's `AgentDeepLink` parser omitted the field, so the browser-copilot persona never reached the agent. Added `extraSystemPrompt` to the parsed type + the `agentCommandFromIngress` dispatch input (which already threads it into the run).
11. **[P1] Explicit relay host pointed at the gateway** (`background.js getRelayHost`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). `getRelayHost` returned the gateway URL's hostname, but the node-owned CDP bridge is a *local loopback* intake (it drives the user's local Chrome); with a remote gateway (per `config.example`'s `wss://…ts.net`) the relay would dial the wrong host. It now defaults to `127.0.0.1`, with an explicit `relayHost` override for advanced setups.
12. **[high] Node-routing fail-open** (`sidepanel.js`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). A failed node turn silently fell back to a direct (unconfined) `sessions.send`, dropping `gateway.tools.byNode` confinement. The relay now records the intake's `nodeIntegrated` (via `/whoami`); `nodeTurn` returns `fallbackAllowed`; the panel **fails closed** (asks to reconnect the node) when a node was hosting — genuine gateway-only deployments still fall back.
13. **[P2] Options page blocked tokenless loopback** (`options.js`) — **fixed** (`c99fd6ab78`). The Options page hard-required a token, mismatching the relay/side-panel tokenless-loopback support (#8); it now allows an empty token for loopback gateways (`127.0.0.1`/`localhost`/`::1`) while still requiring one for remote gateways.
14. **[P0] F3 seam broke runtime module resolution — found via live deploy** — **fixed** (`b3e840c785`). The F3 fix (#3) registered `node-gateway-events-internal` in `plugin-sdk-private-local-only-subpaths.json` — the **test/QA-exclusion bucket** (`ssrf-runtime-internal`, `qa-*`, `*-test-contracts`) that is omitted from the package `exports` map. In any **installed** package the bridge's runtime `import` threw `ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED`, so `ensureExtensionRelayForProfiles` never bound the relay (the error was swallowed by its own `try/catch`) and node-anchored My Browser silently failed. Build, all three boundary checks, and static review passed — the break is runtime-only (the exclusion bucket is also exempt from the subpaths-exported check). Fixed in two steps: first made it a resolvable entrypoint (`b3e840c785`), then **refactored to a true private seam** (`baee5d38a8`) — the node-host injects `emitNodeGatewayEvent` into the bridge in-process via the `onNodeHostStart` hook, so it is **never on the public plugin-SDK surface** (a third-party plugin cannot import it; the surface/api budgets that flagged the exported form now pass for it) and it still throws without a registered node. `emitNodeGatewayEvent` stays removed from the public `gateway-runtime` barrel (the original finding).
15. **[P0] F4 rename left the relay gate on a dead literal — found via live deploy** — **fixed** (`f4f56efa2e`). `ResolvedBrowserConfig.profiles` is `Record<string, BrowserProfileConfig>` — it holds the **raw input** driver, not the mapped resolved driver. So after the `extension → extension-bridge` rename (#5), the relay gate `needsBridge` (`server-lifecycle.ts:39`) compared a raw profile against the now-dead literal `"extension"` and was **always false** — the node-owned CDP bridge never bound, even with #14 fixed. Gate on the input `"extension-bridge"`. (`config.ts:552` and `profile-capabilities.ts:27` are correct — they operate on the mapped/resolved driver.) Verified live: the relay binds on the node-host **eager-start with no kick**, the extension reconnects, and the tab attaches.
16. **[high] Node-turn status failure could bypass confinement** (`sidepanel.js`) — **fixed** (`12b9e03fa1`). The `catch` around the `nodeTurn` message treated a *rejected* `chrome.runtime.sendMessage` (background worker/relay unreachable) the same as a gateway-only deployment and fell through to a direct, unconfined `sessions.send`. The direct fallback now defaults **closed** and is allowed only on a positive gateway-only signal (`fallbackAllowed === true`); a node-hosted intake (`fallbackAllowed === false`) or a rejected message both fail closed, so a node-confined turn never silently drops `gateway.tools.byNode`.
17. **[high] node identity not surfaced on the bridge `/whoami`** (`server-lifecycle.ts`) — **fixed** (`1ce3dccc87`). `ensureExtensionRelayForProfiles` never passed an identity to `ensureExtensionBridge`, so `/whoami` always reported `nodeIntegrated:false`; the extension then treated a node-hosted bridge as gateway-only and allowed the fallback above. The hosting node's identity is now threaded through the DI seam (`runner → onNodeHostStart ctx → registry → ensureExtensionBridge`); verified live: `/whoami` returns `{nodeId, nodeIntegrated:true}` (+ `extension-bridge-server.whoami.test.ts`).
18. **[P1] browser CLI status didn't surface the `extension-bridge` driver** — **fixed** (`c03c0c3585`, refined `fc4fac3a29`). The profile status now surfaces the configured `extension-bridge` driver name (`server-context.ts` reverse-maps the resolved `extension` 1:1; `ProfileStatus.driver` type updated). An initial pass also widened the CLI *create-profile* input to advertise `extension-bridge`/`clawd`, but the create route only accepts `existing-session`, so that advertisement was reverted on review (`fc4fac3a29`) — those profiles are config/toggle-created, not CLI-created. The resolved→public mapping was then extracted into a documented, unit-tested pure function `toPublicProfileDriver` (`31440f233f`); its JSDoc records that a resolved `extension` only ever originates from a configured `extension-bridge` (legacy input `extension` is migrated to `existing-session` by the doctor *before* resolution), so the status stays consistent with the legacy `driver:"extension"` doctor migration with no collision. The same helper was then applied to the second public surface — the `browser status` route + `BrowserStatus` type (`1ec244274e`) — and a sweep of `browser/` confirmed the only remaining raw `extension` driver types are the internal `ResolvedBrowserProfile` + the helper param (correct).
19. **[P2] `extension-bridge` not documented in generated config help** — **fixed** (`c03c0c3585`). The zod `driver` union had no `.describe()`, so the generated config help omitted the driver values; added a description covering `openclaw`/`clawd`/`existing-session`/`extension-bridge`. (Build + config-help-quality test + browser-tool tests green.)
20. **[P1] node→gateway event emitter not re-registered after reconnect** (`runner.ts`) — **fixed** (`fc4fac3a29`). `onClose` nulls the emitter on disconnect (so plugins fail loudly rather than dropping a turn), but it was never re-registered, so a node-originated browser turn after a transient gateway reconnect stranded on a null emitter. Re-registered in `onHelloOk`, which fires on every (re)connect once the hello handshake completes.
21. **[medium] unauthenticated loopback CDP bridge face** (`extension-bridge-server.ts`) — **hardened** (`b3533a10c5`). The `/extension` relay was HMAC-authed, but the loopback CDP face (`/devtools/browser/…`) Playwright connects to was open — a stray local process could discover targets and drive the user's real Chrome. The bridge now registers its token in the bridge-auth registry and requires `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on the `/devtools/browser/` upgrade (constant-time `crypto.timingSafeEqual`); with no token configured it stays trusted-local (back-compat). `getHeadersWithAuth` presents the registered token so the node's `connectOverCDP` authenticates to its own bridge (non-bridge ports are unaffected); `/json` discovery stays open since the per-startup browser guid is the secret. Covered by `extension-bridge-server.cdp-auth.test.ts` and **verified E2E on a live node-anchored deploy — the agent still drives the attached tab to example.org with the CDP face now authenticated (no regression):**

![CDP-face auth E2E — the LLM agent drives the attached tab to example.org ("Done — the tab is now on https://www.example.org/") with the loopback CDP face now requiring the bridge token](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/cdp-auth-e2e.png)
22. **[high] node-gateway emitter exposed on the public plugin SDK** (`src/plugins/types.ts`) — **fixed** (`0913003ee2`, finalized `efa5cbd4c4`). The F3 DI seam put `emitNodeGatewayEvent` on the public `OpenClawPluginNodeHostCommand.onNodeHostStart` ctx, so any plugin author could originate node-attributed gateway events — the private boundary F3 was meant to keep. The emitter is removed from the public `onNodeHostStart` ctx type (which now carries only `nodeId`); the node-host passes it on the **runtime** ctx **only to the bundled node-anchored browser bridge**, gated on the **registry plugin identity** (`entry.pluginId === "browser"`, the bundled plugin's manifest id — not a forgeable self-declared cap; `9a82c1d790`) — every other plugin's startup hook gets just `nodeId`; the emitter stays off the public `{nodeId?}` ctx type (the browser plugin reads it via an internal cast). (Three interim passes — a host-internal `plugin-sdk` subpath, an ungated runtime ctx, then a cap-gated ctx — were superseded to drop a package self-resolution, stop the emitter being visible to all plugin hooks, and gate on identity rather than a declarable cap.) Build + lint + tests green; **verified E2E across deploys** — bridge up, `nodeIntegrated:true`, the gated emitter still reaches the bridge which originates node-attributed turns (the agent drove the attached tab to example.net → example.com → iana.org).
23. **[P2] stale byNode tests (run-scoped contract)** — **fixed** (`b58b000d71`). The byNode tests (from the stacked #93411 base) targeted the retired session-global registry (`setSessionHostingNodeId`) instead of the run-scoped `hostingNodeId`, so they failed against the final implementation: byNode is enforced in `createOpenClawCodingTools` (`agent-tools.ts:932`, `resolveNodeScopedToolPolicy(options.hostingNodeId)`) where `hostingNodeId` is threaded through the run (`attempt.ts → params → agent-tools`); `resolveGatewayScopedTools` is a byNode no-op for non-node MCP/HTTP callers. The embedded test now passes `hostingNodeId` (where byNode bites); the gateway-resolver test is rewritten to a single no-op assertion. Both pass (68/68 + green). *(These are #93411 stacked-base test files — fixed here per request; they reconcile on the #93411 rebase.)*
24. **[medium] broad Chrome extension control surface** (`extensions/chrome-extension/manifest.json`) — **audited; manifest reduced to 10 genuinely-used permissions.** History: an initial narrowing (`4a75987af5`) wrongly removed `declarativeNetRequest` + `clipboardWrite` on a **faulty grep** (escaped `\|` matched a literal pipe → false 0 uses), reverted in `5effcef169`. On proper re-audit: `clipboardWrite` is used (`sidepanel.js:696` writeText) and stays; `declarativeNetRequest` backed **only the dead frame-strip rule**, removed in `3b2374b616` (see finding 26). The remaining 10 are all exercised — `debugger` (CDP, 13), `tabs` (22), `storage` (23), `webNavigation.onCompleted`, `sidePanel` (6), `tabGroups` (3), `alarms` (3), `scripting`, `activeTab`, `clipboardWrite`. Host scope required (bridge WebSockets → loopback/`*.ts.net`; `webNavigation` needs any-URL visibility). No over-broad/unused permission remains; the residual concern is product/security acceptance of CDP-based browser control, not a code change.
25. **[high] emitter gate could be shadowed by plugin id** (`src/node-host/plugin-node-host.ts`) — **fixed** (`5effcef169`). Gating the privileged node→gateway emitter on `entry.pluginId === "browser"` alone let a **config-loaded plugin shadow the bundled `"browser"` id** and receive it. Now gated on **both** the registry plugin's trusted bundled origin **and** id: `entry.origin === "bundled" && entry.pluginId === "browser"` — a config/external plugin is `origin !== "bundled"`, so it is **fail-safe denied**. Threaded the plugin record's trust `origin` (`PluginOrigin`) onto the `PluginNodeHostCommandRegistration` entry (type + the single `registry.ts` push site); the field is optional so non-registry fixtures default to `undefined` (denied). `tsgo:core` clean; `loader` (160) + `plugin-node-host` (2) tests pass; oxlint 0/0. **Re-deployed + E2E re-proven on the hardened gate:** the bundled browser plugin (`origin:"bundled"`) still passes — `node.invoke 2520ms` + `live session turn` 9.9s, agent drove the attached tab to iana.org:

![Emitter origin-gate E2E — after hardening the gate to require origin:"bundled" AND id:"browser", the bundled browser bridge still receives the emitter and the agent drives the attached tab to iana.org (a config plugin shadowing the id would be denied)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-gate-e2e.png)
26. **[high] frame-strip rule strips CSP/X-Frame-Options for no consumer** (`extensions/chrome-extension/background.js`) — **fixed** (`3b2374b616`). The `declarativeNetRequest` dynamic rule removed `X-Frame-Options` + `Content-Security-Policy` response headers for the gateway hostname in `sub_frame`s, per its comment *"so the gateway Control UI loads in the side panel iframe"* — but there is **no `<iframe>` anywhere** in the extension (`sidepanel.html`/`options.html` have none). Dead code that needlessly weakened header security. Removed the rule (`updateFrameStrippingRule` + its `gatewayUrl`-change listener + startup call + `FRAME_STRIP_RULE_ID`) and the now-unused `declarativeNetRequest` permission — eliminating the header-stripping entirely. `getGatewayUrl` stays (used elsewhere); extension oxlint delta 0. **Re-deployed + E2E re-proven:** 10 permissions, no DNR, agent drove the tab to example.org (`node.invoke 225ms` + `live session turn` 7.5s):

![Frame-strip-removal E2E — after deleting the dead frame-strip DNR rule and the declarativeNetRequest permission (10 permissions, no CSP/X-Frame-Options stripping), the agent still drives the attached tab to example.org](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-no-framestrip-e2e.png)
27. **[high] bridge auth used the raw (unresolved) gateway token** (`extensions/browser/src/browser/server-lifecycle.ts`) — **fixed** (`55bf3af73f`). The CDP bridge passed the **raw** `getRuntimeConfig().gateway.auth.token` to the relay/CDP auth gate, so a SecretRef (`${SECRET:…}`) or env-provided gateway token would be HMAC'd as an unresolved literal — or missed entirely — breaking valid SecretRef/env-token setups and calibrating the gate to the wrong value. Now resolves the **effective** token via `ensureGatewayStartupAuth` (materializes SecretRefs + env-aware), the same derivation the gateway authenticates against and the control relay (`control-auth.ts`) already uses. `tsgo:all` clean (0 errors). **E2E re-proven:** the extension still handshakes with the resolved token (Connected) and drives the attached tab to wikipedia.org:

![Bridge auth resolution E2E — after resolving the effective (SecretRef/env-aware) gateway token instead of the raw config literal, the extension still handshakes (Connected) and the agent drives the tab to wikipedia.org](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-auth-e2e.png)
28. **[P2] document/privatize the public `onNodeHostStart` hook** (`src/plugins/types.ts`) — **fixed** (`82f91a8757`). Made it a *documented + tested* plugin-API decision (rather than privatizing): the doc now states `onNodeHostStart` is a deliberate public API, but the privileged node→gateway emitter is **intentionally not on its ctx** (only `nodeId`) — it is delivered out-of-band to the bundled browser bridge gated on trusted bundled origin + identity. Added a unit test: the bundled browser plugin (`origin:"bundled"`, id `"browser"`) receives the emitter; a config-loaded plugin **shadowing** the `"browser"` id with `origin:"external"` does **not** — which also locks the finding-25 origin-gate. 3/3 tests pass; `tsgo:core` clean.
29. **[high] bridge auth ignored password-mode shared secrets** (`extensions/browser/src/browser/server-lifecycle.ts`) — **fixed** (`a4df31cb39`). The finding-27 fix resolved the effective token but used only `.auth.token`, so a **password/trusted-proxy-mode** gateway (whose active shared secret is the *password*) was left auth-less at the relay/CDP gate. Now selects the **mode-active** secret (token for token/none, password for password/trusted-proxy), mirroring `resolveBrowserControlAuth`. Clean `tsgo:all` (0 errors); the bot-specified suites pass (`extension-bridge-server.auth`/`.cdp-auth`, `gateway/auth`, `gateway/startup-auth`).
30. **[P1] side panel sent only the token in the gateway handshake** (`extensions/chrome-extension/sidepanel.js`) — **fixed** (`04b4d10b55`). The client-side complement to findings 27/29: the extension's connect handshake sent `auth: {token}` only, so a **password-mode** gateway rejected the side-panel connection. The gateway accepts `token || password` as the shared secret but the extension can't know the mode, so it now sends the configured credential under **both** fields (the gateway validates only the mode-active one); token mode is unaffected (gateway reads `token` first). Options label updated (token *or* password). **E2E re-proven:** token-mode side panel still connects (Connected) + drives the tab.
31. **[P2] `onNodeHostStart` needed public SDK docs** (`docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md`) — **fixed** (`04b4d10b55`). The hook was documented only in a type comment (finding 28). Added it to the public plugin SDK docs (`api.runtime.nodes`): the optional node-host startup hook for long-lived node-anchored services, and that the privileged node→gateway emitter is deliberately off its public ctx (gated to the bundled browser bridge by trusted origin + identity).

## User-story traceability

How the hardening above protects the headline use case (and the supporting stories):

| User story | Protected by |
|---|---|
| Fill forms in **my own** Chrome session (cookies/logins/tabs) | node-anchored turn routing (Summary block 1); `driver:"extension-bridge"` drives the *attached* tab over the node-local CDP bridge |
| Each tab is its **own isolated session** | per-tab session keys (block 3); proven by the MELON multi-tab isolation test |
| A **stray local process can't hijack my browser** | CDP-face auth (`b3533a10c5`) + effective/active-shared-secret bridge auth (findings **27 + 29**) — the loopback CDP endpoint requires my gateway's active secret |
| Only the **bundled bridge** can originate node-attributed turns as me | emitter origin+id gate (finding **25**); `onNodeHostStart` keeps the emitter off the public ctx (finding **28**) |
| The agent only gets the **tools the node allows** | run-scoped `gateway.tools.byNode` (#93411 base; stale tests fixed in finding **23**) |
| **No silent submit; no over-broad surface** | the agent stops short of submitting (demo above); manifest pared to 10 *used* permissions + dead CSP-stripping rule removed (findings **24 + 26**) |

**Disposition of the remaining (non-code) review items** — all investigated; actioned where a code action exists:
- **Rebase / "clean rebased diff after #93411"** — genuinely blocked, not deferred-by-choice: prerequisite **#93411 is still OPEN**, so its `byNode` primitive is not in `main`. Rebasing onto `main` now would *delete* the primitive #93680 depends on. Actionable only once #93411 merges.
- **Dependency Guard / "remove the dependency graph change"** — **#93680 adds zero npm dependencies.** `extensions/chrome-extension/package.json` declares no `dependencies`/`devDependencies` (only a static-asset build config), so its entire lockfile footprint is one empty importer entry (`extensions/chrome-extension: {}`). The version churn the guard reports is the stacked-base / branch-divergence delta, which resolves on the post-#93411 rebase — there is no #93680-introduced dependency to remove.
- **Config/default surfaces upgrade behavior** — rechecked, all additive/opt-in with no existing-install impact: `browser.priorDefaultProfile` is `z.string().optional()` (undefined on existing installs); `driver:"extension-bridge"` is a *new* schema literal with the default unchanged at `openclaw` and no legacy config carrying it (so no doctor migration is needed); `gateway.tools.byNode` is additive (inert unless a node is configured).

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed:** a usable node-anchored browser copilot — node-confined turns, a persona that actually drives the browser, per-tab sessions, and a bridge that survives heavy real-world pages.
- **Real environment tested:** an OpenClaw gateway built from this branch driving the bundled extension in a headless Chromium (Xvfb + chromium + ffmpeg) container, plus a device-paired loopback node for the byNode path, plus the standalone bridge + browser-tool unit suites.
- **Evidence after fix (live):**
  - `pnpm build` clean (`BUILD_EXIT=0`).
  - **byNode narrowing** a real node turn: `[byNode] allow=[web_search,image] before=5{tts,web_search,web_fetch,image,canvas} after=2{web_search,image}` (run-scoped via threaded `hostingNodeId`).
  - **Heavy-page (page-only filter):** navigating to `account.proton.me/mail/signup` — before, the snapshot timed out at ~47s on ~14 phantom targets; after, `skipped-non-page=2, timeouts=0` and the agent read the live form ("one field: Password").
  - **Tab-context:** asked the current URL with no tools, the agent answered `https://example.org/ — "Example Domain"` from injected context with `0` navigations (no reload).
  - **Per-tab isolation:** tab A told a codeword → tab B asked for it → "I don't have a codeword from you in this conversation."
  - **CDP keepalive:** per-op connect churn dropped 7→2 across a multi-step turn.
  - **FULL node-hop GUI flow (captured):** a paired loopback node hosting the extension bridge + the gateway with `nodes.browser.node`/`byNode`. A side-panel turn drove Chrome to IANA entirely through the node: gateway log shows the turn attributed to the node (`agent … node=ffc1b7d3…`) and the browser tool proxied to it (`node.invoke ✓` ×3 → `browser.proxy`), the agent turn succeeded (`live session turn durationMs=27273`), and the tab rendered `iana.org` ("Internet Assigned Numbers Authority"). Screenshot: the panel header reads "Connected — Internet Assigned N…" with the IANA page driven via the node.
  - **Relay auth gate (F1) — proven live on the deployed `3335ecebd9` build:** with `gateway.auth.token` configured, a WebSocket to `:18792/extension` is rejected (`close 1008`) with no token and with a wrong token, and accepted (server emits `connect.challenge`) only with the correct `deriveRelayToken` HMAC. Backed by 4 unit tests.
  - **Bootstrap-deadlock fix — re-proven on the deployed `3335ecebd9` build:** after killing the node-host (`:18792` → unbound) and restarting it with **no manual relay kick**, the node binds `:18792` at startup and the bridge answers `/json/version`.

Honest note: the live test box runs the **claude-cli** backend, which sources its tools via MCP rather than the gateway's native tool resolver, so I could not re-surface that before→after on this box — the native-runtime capture stands, and byNode coverage of claude-cli backends is a `gateway.tools.byNode` (#93411-primitive) question, not this PR's routing change.

> **Two P0 runtime regressions found via this live deploy** (findings 14–15): the F3 host-internal seam and the F4 driver rename each broke the relay at runtime while passing every static gate (build, the three boundary lints, code review, and the public review bot). A real-behavior deploy on a clean install is what surfaced them.

- **Observed result:** the node-routed turn is node-attributed and byNode-scoped; the persona-driven agent navigates and reads the live tab via the node; heavy pages snapshot instead of timing out; commentary renders as distinct steps; per-tab sessions stay isolated.
- **Bug found + fixed (node bootstrap deadlock):** the node-host **never eagerly started** the extension bridge — it bound `:18792` only lazily inside a `browser.proxy` command, which only originates from an agent turn, which needs the bridge up. Circular → node-anchored browser never worked out of the box. Confirmed: container LISTEN ports were `5900,7799,18789` until a `browser.proxy` was forced through the gateway, after which `:18792` bound and the full hop closed. **Fixed in this PR:** an optional `onNodeHostStart` hook on node-host commands; `runNodeHost` runs registered hooks once after the node→gateway emitter is wired; the browser plugin registers a hook that eager-starts the control service (no-op unless a profile uses `driver:"extension-bridge"`), via a lazy import so core never statically imports the extension. **Validated:** with the fix the node-host binds `:18792` at startup with no kick (`startBrowserControlServiceFromConfig` returns a live state and the bridge answers `/json/version`). `pnpm build` clean. (The captured GUI run pre-dated the build and used a one-time manual kick; the fix removes that need.)
- **What was not tested (honest gap):** the byNode *narrowing* log line is debug-level and not surfaced in this run; node-attribution + `node.invoke` proxying prove the hop, and `byNode` is configured + active. No remaining flow is unproven.
- **Proof limitations / environment constraints:** gateway + node ran as two processes on one host (loopback pairing) in the `oc-nodeanchor` container (Xvfb + chromium + ffmpeg) — the earlier "no VM / disk-blocked" claim is retracted.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm build` clean.
- `extension-bridge-server.agent-request.test.ts` — forwards `agent.request` → `onAgentRequest`, error when no routing, empty-message reject, emitter-failure ack (4/4).
- `browser-tool.test.ts` — pins to the hosting node, explicit `node=` overrides (browser suite passing, no regressions).
- Container GUI behavioral runs (above) for persona / per-tab / tab-context / page-only filter / CDP keepalive.

## Risk checklist

- **User-visible behavior change?** Yes — side panel routes through the node when present (direct-gateway fallback); per-tab sessions; injected tab context; new streaming render + tool-step lines.
- **Config/environment/migration change?** Uses #93411's `gateway.tools.byNode`; no new config.
- **Security/auth/tool-execution change?** Yes — turns become node-attributed so byNode can confine them; browser proxy pinned to the hosting node; persona is injected server-side (not client-supplied). Restriction-only via #93411.
- **Highest-risk areas + mitigations:**
  - *Node origination + reply delivery* → bridge only acks; replies use the existing gateway subscription; direct-turn fallback when no node.
  - *Page-only target filter* → only `page` targets announced; same-origin iframes still reached via the page's frame tree, so nothing is lost.
  - *CDP keepalive* → idle-TTL reconnect + the op self-heals if a cached socket is dead.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** hold for #93411 to merge, then rebase to the extension-only delta (which also drops the unrelated stacked-base lockfile drift).
- **Waiting on:** #93411 merge.
- **Addressed:** node-routing + browser-pin + persona + side-panel UX + bridge reliability wired, built clean; bootstrap-deadlock fixed; all six local-review findings resolved or explained (relay-auth gate + 4 unit tests, token hygiene, narrowed SDK surface, config schema; two non-issues explained); combined node-hop flow captured; **two P0 runtime regressions (F3 export resolution, F4 relay gate) found via live deploy and fixed** (`b3e840c785`, `f4f56efa2e`); on the fixed build the relay binds on the node-host eager-start with **no kick**, the bundled extension reconnects + attaches, and the **LLM agent drives the user's attached tab from side-panel turns** (`wikipedia.org → github.com`, recording embedded). F3's final form is the in-process DI seam (`baee5d38a8`).

target.title: fix(compaction): skip replay-unsafe session sends
target.body:
Fixes #84139.

## Summary

- Filters replay-unsafe `sessions_send` artifacts at the compaction safeguard branch-fallback boundary without dropping safe assistant text from mixed assistant messages.
- Keeps the existing duplicate protections for target-side delivered inter-session turns and source-side already-executed `sessions_send` tool call/result artifacts.
- Adds a focused mixed-content regression proving safe assistant text remains provider-visible while `sessions_send` tool-call blocks and results are removed.

## Origin / follow-up

- N/A — independent root-cause fix for linked issue #84139. The recorded related open PR scan reported `related_open_prs: []` and `scan_error: null`, so this is not following or competing with a broader same-contract PR found by the daily-fix scan.

## Root Cause

- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High. The patch is limited to the compaction safeguard branch-fallback source boundary, has focused regression coverage for the over-filtering case, and has fresh current-head production-hook terminal proof.
- **Root cause:** The root/source invariant at the branch-fallback filter was too coarse: it treated any assistant message containing a replay-unsafe `sessions_send` tool call as entirely unsafe. That removed the already-executed send artifact, but it also dropped safe assistant text from mixed text-plus-tool-call turns, losing useful fallback history during compaction recovery.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The fix keeps replay safety at the source boundary by removing only the `sessions_send` tool-call blocks and their matching tool results before `containsRealConversation()` and summarization see recovered branch history. Assistant messages that still contain safe content remain available for summarization; assistant messages that contain only replay-unsafe send blocks are still removed.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** A compaction that fires during an inter-agent `sessions_send` exchange should not duplicate delivered messages, but it also should not erase safe context from the source session's assistant turn. This keeps both sides of the session-state/message-delivery boundary intact.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth owner is the compaction safeguard's recovered branch-history filter, immediately before recovered messages are classified as real conversation and sent to summarization. The patch preserves that boundary by reusing `isMessagingToolSendAction()` for send-action detection and `toolCallId` linkage for matching results; no downstream summary text filtering or delivery-layer special case is added.
- **Compatibility/default behavior:** No public API, user configuration, schema, protocol, wire/data format, migration, provider routing, session persistence format, message delivery behavior, or default compaction setting changes.
- **Out of scope:** Gateway UI behavior, Mantis/Crabbox automation, third-party channel transport, provider selection, idempotency keys, session storage format, and `sessions_send` execution semantics are intentionally unchanged.
- **Patch quality notes:** The production change remains one source file and one regression file. The new `return true` branches keep non-send blocks, malformed non-tool blocks, and unrelated tool calls; the `return false` branch removes only replay-unsafe `sessions_send` blocks after recording their id for result filtering. The test names use "fallback" because they exercise the existing compaction branch-fallback seam that caused the duplicate/replay behavior.
- **What did NOT change:** Normal compaction preparation, message delivery, `sessions_send` execution, idempotency keys, provider routing, session persistence format, config, schema, API, migration behavior, and default compaction settings are unchanged.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: session-state` and `merge-risk: message-delivery`.
- **Risk explanation:** The patch changes which recovered session-branch entries can become compaction summary input when preparation omitted real conversation content. Over-filtering can lose useful fallback context; under-filtering can replay already-executed send artifacts.
- **Why acceptable:** Current-head proof covers both sides of the boundary: target/source `sessions_send` artifacts do not reach the provider, ordinary safe branch fallback remains provider-visible, and the new regression proves mixed assistant text is preserved while `sessions_send` tool-call blocks and results are removed.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Prevents compaction safeguard branch fallback from reusing already-executed `sessions_send` interactions as fallback history during compaction recovery while preserving safe assistant text in mixed source-session turns.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR worktree at `/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-93293` on head `9ca43f2f454a524c0eb9d9e3726dfc575442961e` after `daily_fix.sh sync-main`. The terminal proof script imports the current PR's production `compactionSafeguardExtension`, compaction provider registry, input provenance normalization, and messaging send-action detection.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
TASK_WORKTREE="/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-93293"
EVIDENCE_DIR="/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93293-evidence"
(cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && NO_COLOR=1 FORCE_COLOR=0 TASK_WORKTREE="$TASK_WORKTREE" pnpm exec tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/proof-84139-sessions-send-compaction-replay.mts")
(cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts)
(cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts)
(cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && git diff --check)
(cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts)
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
[proof-84139] Production compaction safeguard hook proof for PR #93293 / issue #84139.
[proof-84139] Production paths: compactionSafeguardExtension, compaction provider registry, input provenance normalization, and messaging send-action detection.
[compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: no real conversation messages to summarize; writing compaction boundary to suppress re-trigger loop.

=== Target session after sessions_send delivery ===
branch reply count before compaction: 1
compaction provider calls for target branch: 0
provider-visible target reply count: 0
summary wrote empty boundary: true
[compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: using session branch messages after compaction preparation omitted real conversation content.

=== Source session after sessions_send tool execution ===
provider message roles: ["user"]
provider sessions_send artifact count: 0
provider preserved requester ask: true
[compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: using session branch messages after compaction preparation omitted real conversation content.

=== Ordinary safe branch fallback ===
provider message roles: ["user","assistant"]
provider preserved deployment context: true

[proof-84139] All replay-safety and fallback-preservation assertions passed.
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  98 passed (98)
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  101 passed (101)
```

```text
git diff --check
# no output

pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts
# no output
```

- **CI lint note after sync-main:** The previous remote `check-lint` failure was in `src/gateway/server-methods/update.ts`, outside this PR diff. `daily_fix.sh check-main-drift` required `sync-main`; after syncing, the branch contains main's `catch((err: unknown) => ...)` update in that file, and the PR diff is again limited to `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts` and `.test.ts`.
- **Observed result after fix:** The target-session scenario starts with one already-delivered `sessions_send` reply in the session branch, but the production compaction safeguard hook sends zero target-branch messages and zero copies of that reply to the compaction provider. The source-session scenario sends only the requester user message to the provider, with `provider sessions_send artifact count: 0`. The ordinary fallback scenario still sends safe user/assistant branch history to the provider. The new regression additionally proves a mixed assistant message reaches the provider as `user, assistant`, with assistant content reduced to the safe text block and without `sessions_send` or delivered-result content.
- **What was not tested:** No external gateway UI, Mantis, Crabbox, or third-party channel/provider live capture of the timing-dependent two-agent workflow was run. The supplied proof is local terminal/log proof over the production compaction safeguard hook path, production replay-safety contracts, and focused regression tests.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 — `status: waiting on author`:** This update supplies the requested author-side repair and fresh current-head proof; label removal is left to the normal re-review flow rather than manual label mutation.
- **RF-002 — preserve safe assistant text and add regression:** Added `preserves assistant text while filtering sessions_send tool calls as fallback history`, which fails on the old whole-message filter and passes after the block-level filter.
- **RF-003 — avoid dropping mixed assistant turns:** `filterReplayUnsafeMessagingToolCalls()` now removes only replay-unsafe send blocks; safe text content remains in the assistant message.
- **RF-004 — bound over-filtering and under-filtering:** The regression proves safe text is preserved, existing source/target replay tests prove send artifacts are still removed, and the production proof shows ordinary safe branch fallback remains provider-visible.
- **RF-005 — narrow repair:** The change is mechanical and limited to the branch-fallback filter plus one focused regression; no delivery, provider, persistence, config, schema, or public-contract surface changed.
- **RF-006 — `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts:270-274`:** Replaced the whole-assistant-message `continue` with block-level filtering and kept `toolCallId` tracking for matching `sessions_send` tool results.
- **RF-007 — compaction safeguard test:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts` passed with 98 tests.
- **RF-008 — related session tests:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts` passed with 97 tests across 3 files.
- **RF-009 — diff whitespace:** `git diff --check` passed with no output.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts`
- **Scenario locked in:** Empty compaction preparation with session branch fallback filters target-side `inter_session` inputs from `sessions_send`, source-side already-executed `sessions_send` tool call/result entries, and mixed assistant turns where safe text appears beside a replay-unsafe `sessions_send` tool call.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The regression sits at the branch-fallback seam that produced duplicate recovery and over-filtering behavior, without requiring a full multi-agent live run or changing `sessions_send` delivery code.
- **TDD RED proof:** Before the production change, the new mixed-content test failed as expected with `AssertionError: expected [ 'user' ] to deeply equal [ 'user', 'assistant' ]`, proving the old filter dropped safe assistant text.

target.title: feat(skills): report malformed SKILL.md files via `skills lint`
target.body:
## Summary

Malformed SKILL.md directories were silently dropped. `loadSkillsFromDirSafe` now returns skipped dirs with a discriminated reason (parse-error, or missing-required-field naming the field); `status.ts` threads it through, and a new `openclaw skills lint [<path>]` prints a "Failed to load" section and exits non-zero on failures (`--json` supported).

- Files: src/skills/loading/local-loader.ts, src/skills/discovery/status.ts, src/cli/skills-cli.ts
- No `CHANGELOG.md` edit (release-only per `AGENTS.md`).

## Why core (not ClawHub)
Core first-run/CLI UX fix touching `src/skills/loading/local-loader.ts` — per `AGENTS.md` these stay in core (security/core hardening, bundled regressions, missing core APIs), not optional skill bundles routed to ClawHub.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/skills/loading/local-loader.test.ts src/skills/discovery/status.test.ts` => **15 passed**
- `pnpm tsgo:core` (tsconfig.core.json) => clean
- `oxfmt --check` + `oxlint` on touched files => clean

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Authors get a diagnostic for malformed SKILL.md files (naming the missing field) via a new `skills lint` command, instead of silent drops.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS (Darwin 25.3.0); OpenClaw at base 2accfeedc7; Node 22; Vitest via scripts/run-vitest.mjs.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/skills/loading/local-loader.test.ts src/skills/discovery/status.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix:** 15 tests pass, incl. a case where a SKILL.md missing `description` appears in the failed-to-load report naming the specific field.
- **Observed result after fix:** Discriminated load-failure reasons (no sentinels); existing loader consumers only read `.skills`, so the added `skipped` field is non-breaking.
- **What was not tested:** `docs/cli/skills.md` not updated in this PR; the `parse-error` arm is currently defensive (YAML parser rarely throws); full CLI E2E; Linux CI.. Independently reviewed by a separate agent before commit; not yet run through Crabbox/$autoreview.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---

## Real behavior proof (updated — real CLI run)

Ran the built `openclaw skills lint` from source against a fixture skills tree
containing a valid skill, a **nested** group skill missing `description`, and a
skill with malformed YAML frontmatter (paths redacted to `<tmp>`):

**Text mode (malformed tree) — exit 1**
```
Failed to load (2):
  ✗ <tmp>/skills/bad-yaml/SKILL.md  (parse error: Flow sequence in block collection must be sufficiently indented and end with a ] at line 2, column 27:
description: [unterminated
                          ^
)
  ✗ <tmp>/skills/group/broken/SKILL.md  (missing required field: description)
```

**JSON mode (malformed tree) — exit 1**
```json
{
  "roots": ["<tmp>/skills"],
  "failures": [
    { "dir": "<tmp>/skills/bad-yaml", "filePath": "<tmp>/skills/bad-yaml/SKILL.md", "reason": "parse-error", "message": "Flow sequence in block collection must be sufficiently indented and end with a ] ..." },
    { "dir": "<tmp>/skills/group/broken", "filePath": "<tmp>/skills/group/broken/SKILL.md", "reason": "missing-required-field", "field": "description" }
  ]
}
```

**Clean dir (valid skill only) — exit 0**, no output.

This demonstrates: recursive descent into the nested `group/broken` skill, strict
YAML detection (the line parser would have accepted `description: [unterminated`),
text + JSON modes, and the non-zero exit on failure for CI use.

target.title: fix(imessage): stage remote media before plugin dispatch
target.body:
## Summary

- Stage remote inbound media during dispatch before plugin hook and plugin-bound inbound-claim metadata is built.
- Mark successfully staged remote media as already staged so the later agent reply path does not restage the same attachment.
- Add regressions for SCP remote-cache rewriting, plugin-bound Codex claim metadata, and Codex `localImage` turn input.

## Linked context

Closes #91744.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: remote iMessage attachments that start as raw macOS Messages paths are staged before plugin-bound Codex inbound claims are constructed, so the Codex-visible media metadata uses the local remote-cache path instead of `/Users/.../Library/Messages/Attachments/...`.
- Real environment tested: fresh OpenClaw worktree `/Users/andy/openclaw-91744-imessage-codex-staged-path` based on live `upstream/main` `9a1f2022b127f27312eeb7f57e42d477bddf2c19`, using the actual dispatch source, message hook mapper, and Codex turn-input module from this patch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` in the worktree to verify the dispatch ordering in source, build the plugin inbound claim event through the real hook mapper, and convert that event through the real Codex turn-input builder.
- Evidence after fix: terminal output from the runtime command:

```json
{
  "dispatchOrder": "stage_remote_media_for_dispatch before deriveInboundMessageHookContext",
  "claimMediaPath": "/tmp/openclaw-proof/.openclaw/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage/photo.jpg",
  "claimMediaPaths": [
    "/tmp/openclaw-proof/.openclaw/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage/photo.jpg"
  ],
  "codexInput": [
    {
      "type": "text",
      "text": "what is this?",
      "text_elements": []
    },
    {
      "type": "localImage",
      "path": "/tmp/openclaw-proof/.openclaw/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage/photo.jpg"
    },
    {
      "type": "localImage",
      "path": "/tmp/openclaw-proof/.openclaw/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage/photo.jpg"
    }
  ],
  "rawMessagesPathVisible": false,
  "localImagePath": "/tmp/openclaw-proof/.openclaw/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage/photo.jpg"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the dispatch source now stages remote media before `deriveInboundMessageHookContext()`, the plugin inbound claim metadata carries the staged remote-cache path, and the final Codex turn input contains `localImage` entries for that staged path while `rawMessagesPathVisible` is `false`.
- What was not tested: a live remote Mac/iMessage conversation, because this environment does not have live remote Mac/iMessage credentials.

### Current-head proof (2026-06-21, `2e0039a8c3704bd382a4cb40e390a2c0cf191d2a`)

- Behavior addressed: plugin-bound Codex inbound claims still receive staged absolute remote-cache media paths, while unrelated `message_received` hooks no longer trigger SCP/cache staging for ordinary remote iMessage turns.
- Real environment tested: rebased local PR checkout on `origin/main` `bebc5d847d1`, branch head `2e0039a8c37`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts -t "stages remote iMessage media before plugin-bound inbound claim metadata reaches Codex|does not cache-stage remote iMessage media for message_received-only hooks"`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply.stage-sandbox-media.scp-remote-path.test.ts`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --base origin/main`; `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix: focused dispatch tests passed with 2 tests run and 211 skipped; the staging regression suite passed with 5 tests run; autoreview returned `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`; `git diff --check` passed.
- Observed result after fix: the plugin-bound claim test proves `stageSandboxMedia` is called with `remoteMediaMode: "cache"` before `runInboundClaimForPluginOutcome`, and the new message_received-only regression proves `stageSandboxMedia` is not called, `MediaStaged` remains unset, reply fallback keeps the raw path for later get-reply staging, and message hook metadata omits unstaged remote `mediaPath(s)`/`mediaUrl(s)` instead of exposing `/Users/.../Messages/Attachments/...`.
- What was not tested: a live remote Mac/iMessage conversation, because this environment does not have live remote Mac/iMessage credentials.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.message-hooks.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply.stage-sandbox-media.scp-remote-path.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-turn-input.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor.media-policy.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/channel-inbound-roots.contract.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply.stage-sandbox-media.scp-remote-path.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-turn-input.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply.stage-sandbox-media.scp-remote-path.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-turn-input.test.ts`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`

- Dispatch stages remote iMessage media before plugin-bound Codex inbound claim metadata is built.
- Codex turn input uses the staged remote-cache path as `localImage`.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`
- Highest-risk area: remote media staging now runs earlier in dispatch for remote inbound media, before plugin hooks and plugin-bound claims.
- Mitigation: the new dispatch staging is gated on `MediaRemoteHost`, a session key, inbound media, and `!MediaStaged`; successful staging marks `MediaStaged` to preserve the single-stage contract for later agent processing. Existing path policy and size checks remain in `stageSandboxMedia()`.

- Waiting on: normal PR checks and durable ClawSweeper verdict.

target.title: fix(ui): auto-reload Control UI when service worker activates a new version
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

After a gateway update, browsers hold a stale service worker serving an older
Control UI bundle. The old SW passes the old protocol version to the gateway
while the gateway expects the new version, causing a persistent "Protocol
Mismatch" error. Hard-refresh and cache-clear workarounds fail when the SW
intercepts navigation or the user is behind token-based auth (cloud
deployments like Zeabur, Fly, Railway).

Users are completely locked out of their own system after every update until
they manually clear browser site data through DevTools — a workflow that is
especially difficult for non-technical users on cloud deployments where no
local terminal is available.

## Why This Change Was Made

The SW already detects version changes (the build id is embedded in the SW
URL as a `v` query parameter and in the cache name). When a new version
activates, `skipWaiting()` and `clients.claim()` take over immediately —
but open tabs still show the old cached bundle until the user manually
refreshes. This change closes that gap by having the new SW tell the page
to reload itself.

## Changes

- **`ui/public/sw.js`** — post a `{ type: "sw-updated", version }` message to
  all open clients in the `activate` handler so the page knows a new SW
  version is active
- **`ui/src/main.ts`** — listen for the `sw-updated` message on the SW
  registration and call `window.location.reload()` so the Control UI
  immediately picks up assets matching the current gateway protocol version

## Evidence

- Both changes are additive and minimal (no new dependencies, no API
  surface changes)
- The reload only fires on actual SW version changes (the build id
  embedded during Vite build changes only when the package version or git
  SHA changes)
- Existing SW tests cover the activate path; the postMessage call is
  fire-and-forget and a no-op when no clients are listening

## Related

Closes #95292

target.title: perf(models-config): unify auth-profile fingerprint cache + targetProvider short-circuit
target.body:
## Summary

Improves `models.json` preparation so OpenClaw can skip expensive implicit-provider discovery when the relevant inputs are unchanged, while still re-planning when canonical auth state, plugin catalogs, or generated config inputs drift.

This PR reconciles two complementary optimizations inside `ensureOpenClawModelsJson`: a warm-process whole-input fingerprint cache and a provider-scoped `targetProvider` structural short-circuit. Together they make the common model-setup path cheap on warm turns, cold starts, gateway restarts, and unrelated-provider changes without letting stale provider/model routing survive across real input changes.

The key correctness move is that cache keys now read the canonical SQLite agent auth store instead of the retired `auth-profiles.json` file. That means migrated agents no longer look permanently `absent` to the cache, credential changes invalidate the plan when they should, and the runtime avoids the legacy file-hashing attack surface entirely.

## Why

`ensureOpenClawModelsJson` sits on startup and model-selection paths where a full implicit-provider plan can fan out through plugin discovery and provider-catalog work. If the prepared config is already valid, that work is pure overhead; if auth profiles, generated plugin catalogs, or persisted `models.json` inputs changed, reusing the old plan is a routing-integrity bug.

This PR aims for the narrow middle: reuse aggressively only when the canonical inputs prove stable, and fail closed to a full re-plan when they do not. That preserves the performance intent of the earlier branches while tightening the cache boundary around the state OpenClaw actually uses today.

## What Changed

- Adds an in-process whole-input fingerprint cache for warm `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` calls.
- Adds a provider-scoped `targetProvider` structural short-circuit so cold starts, gateway restarts, and unrelated-provider edits can skip full planning when disk/config validation says the scoped output is still valid.
- Fingerprints the canonical SQLite auth store (`openclaw-agent.sqlite`, via the warm pooled handle) instead of legacy `auth-profiles.json`, while continuing to strip volatile OAuth session fields so token rotation does not churn the cache.
- Keeps `type:"token"` expiry fields cache-significant so expired static tokens invalidate the plan.
- Threads generated plugin catalogs into the pre-plan parsed `models.json` and writes them consistently through skip, noop, and write paths.
- Makes the compatibility source-fingerprint shim explicitly non-cacheable for unreadable/corrupt auth state, and has both `model-catalog` and `models list` provider-catalog consumers skip persisted catalog cache read/write when that source is non-cacheable.
- Preserves the hardenings from both predecessor branches: fail-closed `uncacheable` handling, prototype-pollution guards, bounded compare depth, per-model transport refusal, validated-outcome TOCTOU close, and scoped-cache isolation.

## Relationship to Prior PRs

Supersedes #73260 and #73261 with one reconciled implementation. They were complementary, not redundant:

- **#73260** added content-hash auth-profile fingerprinting plus `models.json` drift detection. It was the warm-process whole-input memoizer; OAuth token rotation no longer busts the cache.
- **#73261** added the provider-scoped `targetProvider` structural short-circuit with disk-vs-config validation. It skips discovery on cold start / gateway restart, or when an unrelated provider changed.

Both earlier branches still hashed the legacy JSON auth file, which reads `absent` for agents already migrated to SQLite. This PR moves that shared cache input to the canonical store and carries forward the security and correctness refinements from both branches.

## Verification

Exact-head local verification on `e5671e9b27`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.fingerprint-cache.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts src/commands/models/list.provider-catalog.test.ts` -> **88 passed** across the focused fingerprint/model-catalog/provider-catalog suites.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` -> clean.
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` -> clean.
- `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts` -> all 5 runtime assertions passed.
- `git diff --check` -> clean.

## Real behavior proof

Real-runtime proof script `scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts` drives the real `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` path against a real on-disk agent directory, real generated plugin-catalog sidecars, the real `readyCache` populate/drift cycle, the real no-create auth-store read, and the public `buildModelsJsonSourceFingerprint` shim. **No mocking of the cache/fingerprint seams under test**; only the network edge is avoided with static credentials.

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Generated plugin-catalog sidecar drift must bust the warm models-config cache and reconcile rogue files; fingerprint-only auth reads must not create the SQLite auth DB; plugin-catalog-only plans must not rewrite the root `models.json`; corrupt or malformed SQLite auth state must read as unreadable and make the source fingerprint non-cacheable. The new catalog-cache regressions then pin that both `model-catalog` and provider-catalog consumers skip persisted catalog cache read/write when the source fingerprint is non-cacheable.
- **Real environment tested:** Topic worktree at head `e5671e9b27`, with real temp agent directories, real SQLite files/corruption, real generated plugin-catalog sidecars, and the real source-fingerprint shim.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts`, run from the topic worktree.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured stdout from the live run:

```
[1/5] sidecar-drift-busts-cache-and-reconciles
    rogue sidecar present after re-plan: false
    ok
[2/5] fingerprint-read-does-not-create-agent-db
    auth DB created: false, -wal: false, -shm: false
    ok
[3/5] stable-no-sidecar-still-hits
    models.json mtimeMs cold=1781500389439.0088 warm=1781500389439.0088 ino cold=4067810 warm=4067810
    ok
[4/5] plugin-catalog-only-write-preserves-root
    root models.json ino cold=4067823 warm=4067823 mtimeMs cold=1781500389539.2183 warm=1781500389539.2183
    ok
[5/5] unreadable-auth-store-fails-closed
    no-db outcome.kind=absent
    valid-store outcome.kind=present
    corrupt-db outcome.kind=unreadable
    corrupt-db fingerprint cacheable=false
    malformed-json-cell outcome.kind=unreadable
    malformed-json-cell fingerprint cacheable=false
    ok

All runtime assertions passed.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** All five runtime assertions passed (process exit 0). The source-fingerprint shim now reports `cacheable=false` for both corrupt SQLite and malformed JSON auth-store states, so catalog callers can decline persisted cache keys instead of reusing stale rows under a stable sentinel.
- **What was not tested:** A fully live multi-provider gateway boot is not exercised; the cache populate/drift/reconcile, no-create auth read, plugin-catalog-only write guard, and unreadable-auth fingerprint semantics are driven end-to-end against real on-disk state by the proof script above. Focused unit coverage pins the model-catalog and provider-catalog consumer behavior.

target.title: Add reset family carryover summaries
target.body:
## Behavior or issue addressed

Depends on #90239.

When a session has been reset/rotated, the active transcript can be clean while the previous reset archive still contains the latest compaction summary. The next embedded-agent prompt therefore starts without the carryover context unless the model/tool explicitly asks for family history.

This change reuses the session-family transcript resolver from #90239 and injects the latest reset-archive compaction summary into embedded-agent prompt context before the LLM boundary, but only when the active context does not already contain a compaction summary.

## Fix

- Move the #90239 family/reset transcript target resolver into a shared gateway helper.
- Keep `chat.history(includeFamily)` on the shared resolver.
- Pass the current session store path/entry into embedded-agent attempts.
- Add a prompt-context transform that prepends one `compactionSummary` from reset ancestor transcripts.
- Skip injection when the active session already has a compaction summary.
- Cap family transcript read targets before reading files; ancestor targets stop at the cap while current-session targets remain available.
- Rebased onto current `origin/main` and preserved the newer transcript-reader seam plus same-session reset fallback.

## Real behavior proof

**Current head proven:** `90525a0927bfcd94912dfa9c3d702c6de6cb231d`.

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A reset/rotated logical session should read bounded reset-family history through the shared resolver, keep current reset archive plus current active transcript available inside the cap, inject exactly one reset-family compaction summary before the embedded-agent LLM boundary, and cap oversized archived summaries before prompt injection.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch at `/home/clever/.openclaw/workspace/github/openclaw`, using current branch TypeScript modules and temporary on-disk session stores/transcripts under `/tmp/openclaw-family-proof-905-*`. No private channel, endpoint, phone, prompt, or auth data was used.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
$ git rev-parse HEAD
90525a0927bfcd94912dfa9c3d702c6de6cb231d

$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'TS'
# The script imported these current-branch modules:
# - ./src/gateway/session-history-family.ts
# - ./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-family-carryover.ts
# It created a temporary sessions.json, 35 ancestor transcripts,
# one current reset archive transcript, and one current active transcript.
# Ancestor 29 contained an oversized compaction summary larger than
# CARRYOVER_SUMMARY_MAX_CHARS. The script called the real family target
# resolver, real carryover resolver, installed the prompt transform,
# applied the transform twice, printed the observed result, and removed temp files.
TS
{
  "head": "90525a0927bfcd94912dfa9c3d702c6de6cb231d",
  "targetCount": 32,
  "firstTargets": [
    "ancestor-proof-00:ancestor",
    "ancestor-proof-01:ancestor",
    "ancestor-proof-02:ancestor"
  ],
  "lastTargets": [
    "ancestor-proof-29:ancestor",
    "current-proof-session:reset-archive",
    "current-proof-session:active"
  ],
  "currentResetTargetPresent": true,
  "currentActiveLast": true,
  "ancestor29Present": true,
  "ancestor30Present": false,
  "ancestor34Present": false,
  "carryoverSummaryLength": 16384,
  "carryoverSummaryMaxChars": 16384,
  "carryoverSummaryEndsTruncated": true,
  "carryoverSummaryPrefix": "Exact-head 905 oversized carryover 29: x",
  "carryoverTokensBefore": 2029,
  "promptRolesBeforeLlm": [
    "compactionSummary",
    "user"
  ],
  "compactionSummaryCount": 1,
  "idempotentRoles": [
    "compactionSummary",
    "user"
  ]
}
```

**Evidence after fix:**

- The shared resolver returned 32 targets and kept both current reset archive and current active transcript inside the cap.
- The current active transcript was ordered last for final history trimming.
- Ancestors beyond the reserved cap were not read: `ancestor30Present` and `ancestor34Present` are false.
- Carryover selected the latest ancestor inside the capped family window, `ancestor-proof-29`, and preserved its token count.
- The oversized archived compaction summary was capped before prompt injection: `carryoverSummaryLength` equals `carryoverSummaryMaxChars` (16384) and `carryoverSummaryEndsTruncated` is true.
- The embedded prompt transform injected exactly one `compactionSummary` before the model boundary and remained idempotent on a second pass.

**Observed result after fix:** The terminal output showed `targetCount: 32`, current reset archive plus current active transcript retained at the end of the target list, `ancestor30Present: false`, `ancestor34Present: false`, one pre-LLM `compactionSummary`, idempotent second transform roles, and an oversized summary capped exactly at 16384 chars with `[summary truncated]`.

**Validation on current head:**

```text
$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-models.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  20 passed (20)

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-family-carryover.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  8 passed (8)

$ git diff --check
# no output

$ CI=true node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).

$ CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json
# passed
```

**What was not tested:** Full live Telegram/Slack/Discord end-to-end reset turn was not run locally; the proof exercises the real resolver and embedded carryover path against on-disk OpenClaw-like session artifacts.
